info.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder
info.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder.
info.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
info.statement: The viral activity level of Overarching Opioid use disorder in wastewater is {value}.

info.long_name: Medicaid and CHIP Injury and Overdose Rate
info.short_description: Percentage of Medicaid beneficiaries with injury and overdose related measures.
info.long_description: State-level injury and overdose rates for Medicaid beneficiaries, from the Medicaid and CHIP Adult Core Set Quality Measures voluntarily reported by states to CMS (2014–2023). Includes opioid use disorder (OUD: percent of adults without cancer receiving high-dosage opioid prescriptions), initiation and engagement of substance use disorder treatment (IET: percent of adults with a new substance use disorder diagnosis who initiated and engaged in treatment), follow-up after ED visit for alcohol and drug abuse (FUA: percent of adults with a follow-up visit within 30 days after an ED visit for alcohol or drug abuse), and concurrent use of opioids and benzodiazepines (COB: percent of adults who received concurrent prescriptions for opioids and benzodiazepines). Reporting is voluntary; not all states report all measures each year.

info.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder
info.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder.
info.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
info.statement: The viral activity level of Overarching Opioid use disorder in wastewater is {value}.

info.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder
info.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder.
info.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Overarching Opioid use disorder. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
info.statement: The viral activity level of Overarching Opioid use disorder in wastewater is {value}.

bundle_injury_overdose/dist/medicaid_inj.long_name: Medicaid and CHIP Injury and Overdose Rate
bundle_injury_overdose/dist/medicaid_inj.short_description: Percentage of Medicaid beneficiaries with injury and overdose related measures.
bundle_injury_overdose/dist/medicaid_inj.long_description: State-level injury and overdose rates for Medicaid beneficiaries, from the Medicaid and CHIP Adult Core Set Quality Measures voluntarily reported by states to CMS (2014–2023). Includes opioid use disorder (OUD: percent of adults without cancer receiving high-dosage opioid prescriptions), initiation and engagement of substance use disorder treatment (IET: percent of adults with a new substance use disorder diagnosis who initiated and engaged in treatment), follow-up after ED visit for alcohol and drug abuse (FUA: percent of adults with a follow-up visit within 30 days after an ED visit for alcohol or drug abuse), and concurrent use of opioids and benzodiazepines (COB: percent of adults who received concurrent prescriptions for opioids and benzodiazepines). Reporting is voluntary; not all states report all measures each year.
